name: Wiley Nickel
subject: Republicans are rigging the political process
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
Voters are tired of partisan gerrymandering, and I am, too. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902320/518192398/-488448398?refcode=AU_GWN_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20241218_AM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDc5YjA4Zi01ZGJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UPDL5qVkk_EqvemT2HBpmkXKfD8vOUncsUS83B_T2Po=)
---
Peter, **Voters, not politicians, should choose who represents them.** But in too many places across the country, including in North Carolina, this is no longer the case. For far too long, Republicans have weaponized the undemocratic procedure known as partisan gerrymandering to rig the system and lock themselves into power — and the consequences have been felt nationwide. Congress is as polarized as ever. Too many politicians are more concerned with scoring political points than getting things done for their constituents. That’s because they don’t have to worry about being voted out of office. And the government we have today is not what we’d have if our congressional maps were drawn fairly. **Enough is enough, Peter.** Voters are tired of politicians who play political games to rig the maps and ignore the voices of the American people — and I’m tired of it, too. That’s why I’m determined to do something about it. [**Will you support my work to end this undemocratic process and return political power to the people by adding your name to my petition?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902321/518192400/201338876?refcode=AU_GWN_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20241218_AM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDc5YjA4Zi01ZGJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UPDL5qVkk_EqvemT2HBpmkXKfD8vOUncsUS83B_T2Po=&emci=19bda289-5dbd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=4479b08f-5dbd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=28008880) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902322/518192402/201338876?refcode=AU_GWN_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20241218_AM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NDc5YjA4Zi01ZGJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UPDL5qVkk_EqvemT2HBpmkXKfD8vOUncsUS83B_T2Po=&emci=19bda289-5dbd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=4479b08f-5dbd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=28008880)

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Trump’s DOJ came for me — guess what they found?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Ziltch, nada, goose eggs |
---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902555/518193187/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTE2ZTY0OS01ZWJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FLvMciBcZKXw1m5egi7JOigkx8T5WMZB_yvioMaJ104=&emci=b92478d1-f0bb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=ce16e649-5ebd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=7878003)
Friend, if you thought Trump was abusing power last time he was president, you were right. **The Inspector General just declassified a report showing in 2017,_without_ having any cause, Trump's DOJ searched my phone records and emails.** Shocking, they found nothing. But they were certainly out to get me and will do much worse against their enemies in this coming term. This wasn't about justice. It was about punishing those who dared to hold Trump accountable. It doesn't matter how many intimidation tactics they throw at me, I will never back down from holding this administration accountable. **[Will you chip in $15 or more to help me keep fighting?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902556/518193188/1154900510?refcode=E11780-20241218-TrumpAttacks-SL-Trump&&&&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTE2ZTY0OS01ZWJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FLvMciBcZKXw1m5egi7JOigkx8T5WMZB_yvioMaJ104=)** | [JOIN THE FIGHT »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902557/518193189/828790511?refcode=E11780-20241218-TrumpAttacks-SB-Trump&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTE2ZTY0OS01ZWJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FLvMciBcZKXw1m5egi7JOigkx8T5WMZB_yvioMaJ104=)
---
**Look, if Trump wants to see pictures of my crazy kids and 49ers memes, he should just follow me on Instagram, not violate my Constitutional rights.**
**[Trump and his goons will stop at nothing to try to silence voices like mine in Congress because they don’t want anyone to challenge their power. We know this search is just a taste of what they have in store in this next administration — and that’s why we have to stand together to fight back. Rush a donation right now to show Trump that we won’t back down.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98902558/518193190/1154900510?refcode=E11780-20241218-TrumpAttacks-SL-Trump&&&&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTE2ZTY0OS01ZWJkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FLvMciBcZKXw1m5egi7JOigkx8T5WMZB_yvioMaJ104=)**

Friend, if you thought Trump was abusing power last time he was president, you were right. The Inspector General just declassified a report showing in 2017,without having any cause, Trump's DOJ searched my phone records and emails. Shocking, they found nothing. But they were certainly out to get me and will do much worse against their enemies in this coming term. This wasn't about justice. It was about punishing those who dared to hold Trump accountable. name: Jim Jordan
subject: Just 1 SEAT?!
body:
Our new majority is on the brink.
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=2d4e3547df4d4b79851c0a2433d7cbbd&_e=RU5gGrHgsD79QnN7lRL7SeG9xjcaXh66pCRTGIwbu-x2KFLryp6e_EwA1Ij5hezY_r0bdMiNpcDjZfseu6XzymmKNJQYZKVYb7wrzwLPomaarvp4I4rJaPgYQG5lIUOEtUlz292pXSYShdRFd_5xXQcdpQzKrNWMe2O5Fgo0zm4FPpN1KrMb5neZNFUSyJ_iO2w4vp26_qRMFk-f_BjdJKwIkhSGzBkERZaYK-uAWG0ISCEqlxX0VeuvbYSY3wZ5VWeXka_EgAnBhme1uyV0u2-U5b4NY5nyqVDe0AkHolZ-k3NE8rH3mLVArLNwL8nRIhf2tb07qcHnD-TsPArxa6Ly-rsomUNfCBPcBjaSpHOgZWq58_SkbRgbKx8ogIDeWi7YwfEnhDgQoR5yoMH9A9oZQjrBFrmmFNG7BrhY6EY%3D)
---
Peter,
---
Please don’t misunderstand...
---
I don’t want this email to come across as a message of doom and gloom.
---
_After all, no one can deny the sweeping mandate that President Trump and Republicans were just given by voters like YOU, sweeping the White House _AND_ both chambers of Congress. _
---
But we also must face a glaring reality:
---
**Very soon – with a number of special elections already on the books for 2025 – our new majority will be narrowed to just 1 SEAT.**
---
Yes, you read that right. _1 seat._
---
I know how hard you fought for this new majority, Peter. I’m right there with you...
---
**Over the course of the election, I transferred _$2.5 million_ from my own campaign to help counter Kamala’s plot to buy our House majority.**
---
And now that our new majority could be hanging on by a thread next year, I refuse to let those efforts go to waste.
---
That’s why I have no choice but to reach out before year’s end.
---
I’m asking **_2.5 million patriots_** to match my 2024 commitment with a contribution of ANY amount to help defend our NEW majority that will soon be cut down to just 1 SEAT.
---
[**Please make a grassroots contribution of any size to match my commitment to DEFEND our House majority and SAVE AMERICA.**](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=2d4e3547df4d4b79851c0a2433d7cbbd&_e=RU5gGrHgsD79QnN7lRL7SeG9xjcaXh66pCRTGIwbu-x2KFLryp6e_EwA1Ij5hezY_r0bdMiNpcDjZfseu6XzymmKNJQYZKVYb7wrzwLPomaarvp4I4rJaPgYQG5lIUOEtUlz292pXSYShdRFd_5xXQcdpQzKrNWMe2O5Fgo0zm4FPpN1KrMb5neZNFUSyJ_iO2w4vp26_qRMFk-f_BjdJKwIkhSGzBkERZaYK-uAWG0ISCEqlxX0VeuvbYSY3wZ5VWeXka_EgAnBhme1uyV0u8ri8E-u5t-Sg2eaboUa_-GgEkK44pVHzbSzHJoebX48DeLgn-rSwCIx1MrXAmUMy2QodhW995yKoteE3m1sJ6EKjr0pETAb4wOsHGKdZ4TW6ggEyF9R6b-CQ80xQloC-Jb5wHf7n0zGiM5TpXOmjPc%3D)

clean_body: Our new majority is on the brink. | Peter, Please don’t misunderstand... I don’t want this email to come across as a message of doom and gloom. After all, no one can deny the sweeping mandate that President Trump and Republicans were just given by voters like YOU, sweeping the White House AND both chambers of Congress. But we also must face a glaring reality: Very soon – with a number of special elections already on the books for 2025 – our new majority will be narrowed to just 1 SEAT. Yes, you read that right. 1 seat. I know how hard you fought for this new majority, Peter. I’m right there with you... Over the course of the election, I transferred $2.5 million from my own campaign to help counter Kamala’s plot to buy our House majority. And now that our new majority could be hanging on by a thread next year, I refuse to let those efforts go to waste. That’s why I have no choice but to reach out before year’s end. I’m asking 2.5 million patriots to match my 2024 commitment with a contribution of ANY amount to help defend our NEW majority that will soon be cut down to just 1 SEAT. Thank you, Jim Jordan Paid for by Team Jordan a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed between Jim Jordan for Congress, Buckeye Liberty Fund, and American Liberty Foundation PAC. PO Box 909, Marysville, OH 43040 |

| | Dear Peter, My name is Dan Rayfield and I am thrilled to introduce myself as Oregon’s next Attorney General. As the former Speaker of the Oregon House, I’ve worked with OLCV for years. I know from experience that OLCV is extremely effective at what they do – elect environmental champions, protect Oregon’s natural legacy, and hold our elected officials accountable. That’s why I’m writing to you – will you help ensure that OLCV has the resources they need to continue their important work in 2025 and beyond by making a donation today?

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98904671/518199509/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjllYzM0YzgyLTYxYmQtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMGQzYTlkNTg0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=sLQRLLaxZL_jhml13n1oJZZCeZSmkLDeDrZw18iDAwU=&emci=9c2d529b-1dbc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=9ec34c82-61bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=2107230)

| | Part of what makes OLCV so effective is their strong grassroots member base – which I’m sure you already know if you are receiving this email. You know all the work you’ve helped OLCV accomplish by making a contribution, taking action, or volunteering your time. It’s comforting to know that I have such an effective group to work together with to protect Oregon’s natural legacy, and I’m sure you feel the same. The reason I’m writing to you all today is to remind you that OLCV does not exist without your support. OLCV has a strong record of getting the job done. They work hard to elect pro-environment candidates and hold them accountable, but that work begins with you. [**Will you consider making a gift to OLCV so they can continue to get the job done?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98904674/518199511/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjllYzM0YzgyLTYxYmQtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMGQzYTlkNTg0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=sLQRLLaxZL_jhml13n1oJZZCeZSmkLDeDrZw18iDAwU=&emci=9c2d529b-1dbc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=9ec34c82-61bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=2107230)
---
[**DONATE NOW**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/98904675/518199513/-1695911484?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjllYzM0YzgyLTYxYmQtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMGQzYTlkNTg0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=sLQRLLaxZL_jhml13n1oJZZCeZSmkLDeDrZw18iDAwU=&emci=9c2d529b-1dbc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=9ec34c82-61bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=2107230)

As Oregon’s Attorney General, I’ll work hard over the next four years to defend against attacks from the Trump administration. You can count on me to do that with OLCV’s support, but OLCV can’t do it without you. clean_body: | | | | | | | Dear Peter, My name is Dan Rayfield and I am thrilled to introduce myself as Oregon’s next Attorney General. As the former Speaker of the Oregon House, I’ve worked with OLCV for years. I know from experience that OLCV is extremely effective at what they do – elect environmental champions, protect Oregon’s natural legacy, and hold our elected officials accountable. That’s why I’m writing to you – will you help ensure that OLCV has the resources they need to continue their important work in 2025 and beyond by making a donation today? | | | Part of what makes OLCV so effective is their strong grassroots member base – which I’m sure you already know if you are receiving this email. You know all the work you’ve helped OLCV accomplish by making a contribution, taking action, or volunteering your time. It’s comforting to know that I have such an effective group to work together with to protect Oregon’s natural legacy, and I’m sure you feel the same. The reason I’m writing to you all today is to remind you that OLCV does not exist without your support. OLCV has a strong record of getting the job done. They work hard to elect pro-environment candidates and hold them accountable, but that work begins with you. As Oregon’s Attorney General, I’ll work hard over the next four years to defend against attacks from the Trump administration. You can count on me to do that with OLCV’s support, but OLCV can’t do it without you. * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas man convicted of killing protester before being pardoned now back in court,** " KVUE's **Britny Eubank** -- "A case involving a Texas man [pardoned by the governor](https://substack.com/redirect/24095d0e-55e7-4e36-998e-087375a1d6d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) earlier this year was back in a Travis County courtroom on Tuesday. His attorneys are fighting to throw out an indictment for a separate misdemeanor charge he's facing.

Earlier this year, Gov. **Greg Abbott** pardoned **Daniel Perry** after he was convicted of murder for shooting and killing **Garrett Foster** during a Black Lives Matter protest in 2020. But the pardon didn't end all of his legal troubles.

Perry still faces a misdemeanor charge of deadly conduct for his alleged actions on the night of the shooting leading up to Foster's death. Under Texas law, a deadly conduct charge is when a person knowingly discharges a firearm at someone or a building.

If convicted of the deadly conduct charge, Perry faces up to a year in prison. However, he did spend a little more than a year in prison before he was pardoned, so the judge may take that time served into consideration.

In court Tuesday, Perry's attorneys made two motions - one to quash the indictment for the misdemeanor charge and another to dismiss it. They're trying to make sure Perry is not tried for something he was already pardoned for.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/1f6b3958-c1ae-4ca8-b61f-37665476a284?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This misdemeanor charge is tied to Perry's actions of driving through the crowd of protesters and shooting on July 25, 2020, not specifically Foster's death. His defense argues that because the indictment doesn't specifically name the pedestrians who were in danger in the area, the judge should scrap it.

His attorneys claim they won't be able to give Perry a sufficient defense without knowing whom he's specifically charged with endangering. They also argue that without a specific identification of these pedestrians, it could open Perry up to be being charged for something he's already been tried and acquitted for.

Prosecutors argue that that specific names don't matter because Perry's conduct is what put people in danger. Perry's attorneys argued back that Perry's constitutional right to be notified of these pedestrians' names so he doesn't face a double jeopardy conviction trumps whether the indictment is conduct based or not.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/1f6b3958-c1ae-4ca8-b61f-37665476a284?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The judge expects to make a decision on the defense's motion to quash the indictment by the end of the week. If he decides not to quash it, there could be another hearing in this case on Jan. 9 for the motion to dismiss the indictment.

In that motion to dismiss the indictment, Perry's defense is hoping to have a hearing so they can bring in **David Fugitt** , the lead investigator with the Austin Police Department for Perry's case, to testify. They believe his testimony will be relevant to the decision of throwing out the indictment because in his affidavit, he claimed that the District Attorney's Office tampered with him as a witness and asked him to remove exculpatory information he was planning to present to the grand jury in this case." [KVUE](https://substack.com/redirect/08813552-933e-4cbc-85c0-0875555bb952?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "Texas man convicted of killing protester before being pardoned now back in court, " KVUE's Britny Eubank -- "A case involving a Texas man earlier this year was back in a Travis County courtroom on Tuesday. His attorneys are fighting to throw out an indictment for a separate misdemeanor charge he's facing. Earlier this year, Gov. Greg Abbott pardoned Daniel Perry after he was convicted of murder for shooting and killing Garrett Foster during a Black Lives Matter protest in 2020. But the pardon didn't end all of his legal troubles. Perry still faces a misdemeanor charge of deadly conduct for his alleged actions on the night of the shooting leading up to Foster's death. Under Texas law, a deadly conduct charge is when a person knowingly discharges a firearm at someone or a building. If convicted of the deadly conduct charge, Perry faces up to a year in prison. However, he did spend a little more than a year in prison before he was pardoned, so the judge may take that time served into consideration. In court Tuesday, Perry's attorneys made two motions - one to quash the indictment for the misdemeanor charge and another to dismiss it. They're trying to make sure Perry is not tried for something he was already pardoned for. This misdemeanor charge is tied to Perry's actions of driving through the crowd of protesters and shooting on July 25, 2020, not specifically Foster's death. His defense argues that because the indictment doesn't specifically name the pedestrians who were in danger in the area, the judge should scrap it. His attorneys claim they won't be able to give Perry a sufficient defense without knowing whom he's specifically charged with endangering. They also argue that without a specific identification of these pedestrians, it could open Perry up to be being charged for something he's already been tried and acquitted for. Prosecutors argue that that specific names don't matter because Perry's conduct is what put people in danger. Perry's attorneys argued back that Perry's constitutional right to be notified of these pedestrians' names so he doesn't face a double jeopardy conviction trumps whether the indictment is conduct based or not. The judge expects to make a decision on the defense's motion to quash the indictment by the end of the week. If he decides not to quash it, there could be another hearing in this case on Jan. 9 for the motion to dismiss the indictment. In that motion to dismiss the indictment, Perry's defense is hoping to have a hearing so they can bring in David Fugitt , the lead investigator with the Austin Police Department for Perry's case, to testify. They believe his testimony will be relevant to the decision of throwing out the indictment because in his affidavit, he claimed that the District Attorney's Office tampered with him as a witness and asked him to remove exculpatory information he was planning to present to the grand jury in this case." * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas man convicted of killing protester before being pardoned now back in court,** " KVUE's **Britny Eubank** -- "A case involving a Texas man [pardoned by the governor](https://substack.com/redirect/ed2329bb-e160-4412-bd34-4eabd501f979?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) earlier this year was back in a Travis County courtroom on Tuesday. His attorneys are fighting to throw out an indictment for a separate misdemeanor charge he's facing.

Earlier this year, Gov. **Greg Abbott** pardoned **Daniel Perry** after he was convicted of murder for shooting and killing **Garrett Foster** during a Black Lives Matter protest in 2020. But the pardon didn't end all of his legal troubles.

Perry still faces a misdemeanor charge of deadly conduct for his alleged actions on the night of the shooting leading up to Foster's death. Under Texas law, a deadly conduct charge is when a person knowingly discharges a firearm at someone or a building.

If convicted of the deadly conduct charge, Perry faces up to a year in prison. However, he did spend a little more than a year in prison before he was pardoned, so the judge may take that time served into consideration.

In court Tuesday, Perry's attorneys made two motions - one to quash the indictment for the misdemeanor charge and another to dismiss it. They're trying to make sure Perry is not tried for something he was already pardoned for.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/2db19254-7d4f-4e4c-80f5-4332dc3030da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This misdemeanor charge is tied to Perry's actions of driving through the crowd of protesters and shooting on July 25, 2020, not specifically Foster's death. His defense argues that because the indictment doesn't specifically name the pedestrians who were in danger in the area, the judge should scrap it.

His attorneys claim they won't be able to give Perry a sufficient defense without knowing whom he's specifically charged with endangering. They also argue that without a specific identification of these pedestrians, it could open Perry up to be being charged for something he's already been tried and acquitted for.

Prosecutors argue that that specific names don't matter because Perry's conduct is what put people in danger. Perry's attorneys argued back that Perry's constitutional right to be notified of these pedestrians' names so he doesn't face a double jeopardy conviction trumps whether the indictment is conduct based or not.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/2db19254-7d4f-4e4c-80f5-4332dc3030da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The judge expects to make a decision on the defense's motion to quash the indictment by the end of the week. If he decides not to quash it, there could be another hearing in this case on Jan. 9 for the motion to dismiss the indictment.

In that motion to dismiss the indictment, Perry's defense is hoping to have a hearing so they can bring in **David Fugitt** , the lead investigator with the Austin Police Department for Perry's case, to testify. They believe his testimony will be relevant to the decision of throwing out the indictment because in his affidavit, he claimed that the District Attorney's Office tampered with him as a witness and asked him to remove exculpatory information he was planning to present to the grand jury in this case." * **TOP NEWS**

“**Texas man convicted of killing protester before being pardoned now back in court,** ” KVUE's **Britny Eubank** — “A case involving a Texas man [pardoned by the governor](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eBcypIq7xTSGFhqbYax6WkTOZ35lVOT9iQmQPj21idy07w-J3iYK8mz7hExFW8GDCMeY7iKAiLrhFkYdgU-UyqyTVlw4jRdpSRCTiymDXXwppfvhgAU3P296oMIfJbtPtd2PreRCaltQo39jl3I5UU4RwcX5VNFaAwig5XWfZB4EYzQSvhyet26_1OHmoT0-pVMFzo5qhae2i-upOS16H5sGiWHBtoAuZ3pemPZqfsgal9p120fw28Xy_ZvturZGSfeA9mvkBR9YNV6xAI2v-IU_SmtLjgB-ebDATgmm0mPFIg-aHoymfM1wQ0K3APJW0VqUBTfyXdA9Xz9_KGz6u2PczA-dYV8ZA_Y0XPHhNK0akKDmCvFaWFftxzdSNp49o01jzjFSW77RyhwJ-K519kjwO0AwbI7baWkxZbZREY5PcbKFKTt_1XiZfpfmMS99pO9GzdejwdhhxQj0hHTRQ3Xl538OtlpNAyiaJ7BhUlT4jW7TfwgRHVHkqBg3QkgyqQ/4ce/ieZUP6atQf689rw8isIxCw/h9/h001.SAWzjAx76Sg7GZ6xnJPiHJILkrDfWVNSianD4nZtL_A) earlier this year was back in a Travis County courtroom on Tuesday. His attorneys are fighting to throw out an indictment for a separate misdemeanor charge he's facing.

Earlier this year, Gov. **Greg Abbott** pardoned **Daniel Perry** after he was convicted of murder for shooting and killing **Garrett Foster** during a Black Lives Matter protest in 2020. But the pardon didn't end all of his legal troubles.

Perry still faces a misdemeanor charge of deadly conduct for his alleged actions on the night of the shooting leading up to Foster's death. Under Texas law, a deadly conduct charge is when a person knowingly discharges a firearm at someone or a building.

If convicted of the deadly conduct charge, Perry faces up to a year in prison. However, he did spend a little more than a year in prison before he was pardoned, so the judge may take that time served into consideration.

In court Tuesday, Perry's attorneys made two motions – one to quash the indictment for the misdemeanor charge and another to dismiss it. They're trying to make sure Perry is not tried for something he was already pardoned for.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbGvyVtR_Fo8NUQn1J-2B2znzbbT9lE5wqwCpk8bGipVAWvUsOu1BOt5FR3lUOje6GifC0kbhdAJ6Gt7AAmRGpaCa2STDA0-_g_qgvCj5Z7jtNO1rTtOgZX3OMUkrYU6pzT8qfFezVAIf_TX_cZ_KzxQx3uL-dBBnwyzjNaWOEH2hwkNSZJ8UqEOm8PPB57ywMdIlyZVGKCFlzh132yjLMQ7eOuBaXjeQtd-2uAgEGZGGcsJ_3OnU473f0hIXDG4Sw8Jaw4sqFfAvE90o2afudpE/4ce/ieZUP6atQf689rw8isIxCw/h10/h001.9NNBrfB4sYgrmhBRZKFzCA051-3LBjd6M6hrtarwqIA)
This misdemeanor charge is tied to Perry's actions of driving through the crowd of protesters and shooting on July 25, 2020, not specifically Foster's death. His defense argues that because the indictment doesn't specifically name the pedestrians who were in danger in the area, the judge should scrap it.

His attorneys claim they won't be able to give Perry a sufficient defense without knowing whom he's specifically charged with endangering. They also argue that without a specific identification of these pedestrians, it could open Perry up to be being charged for something he's already been tried and acquitted for.

Prosecutors argue that that specific names don't matter because Perry's conduct is what put people in danger. Perry's attorneys argued back that Perry's constitutional right to be notified of these pedestrians' names so he doesn't face a double jeopardy conviction trumps whether the indictment is conduct based or not.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbGvyVtR_Fo8NUQn1J-2B2znzbbT9lE5wqwCpk8bGipVAWvUsOu1BOt5FR3lUOje6GifC0kbhdAJ6Gt7AAmRGpaCa2STDA0-_g_qgvCj5Z7jtNO1rTtOgZX3OMUkrYU6pzT8qfFezVAIf_TX_cZ_KzxQx3uL-dBBnwyzjNaWOEH2hwkNSZJ8UqEOm8PPB57ywMdIlyZVGKCFlzh132yjLMQ6IACOZ6wLZCwY5IsGHU8KV4ZLfnzenbAqQQs0lcUArn-qtJZ10TRKZFGnyuNsF47c/4ce/ieZUP6atQf689rw8isIxCw/h11/h001.kYNriIbGxByV6d8h-XMo1iAL45oIOhNEgbWw5bayS2w)
The judge expects to make a decision on the defense's motion to quash the indictment by the end of the week. If he decides not to quash it, there could be another hearing in this case on Jan. 9 for the motion to dismiss the indictment.

In that motion to dismiss the indictment, Perry's defense is hoping to have a hearing so they can bring in **David Fugitt** , the lead investigator with the Austin Police Department for Perry's case, to testify. They believe his testimony will be relevant to the decision of throwing out the indictment because in his affidavit, he claimed that the District Attorney's Office tampered with him as a witness and asked him to remove exculpatory information he was planning to present to the grand jury in this case." [KVUE](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eBcypIq7xTSGFhqbYax6WkRSGI7-P8SbJfgr8_f3y-s91R_7TdDtQ-9czNiIprA7gEK9Ukfp7lusMgDoOPcSAliyOXZ7mImEdCiPtrD0X40CnJPeKRoJ8oEvNzB-8aIMCAB-_lMZ6TMfgGeogxE1hQOebgqnTEGYOsoKdWuyFH0qbtx5DlpmcU_lgLXpAgEgttcCxap8CTdUw-5hqZHVgSmjy0rlBcDmCUv9iaE3A-Q-sJ5ZQ4q61i69tWRVYo4t8NhdQ9hIpiCV3aqQsR-5LT1OHAf_ZTkl9OenYA3pStwD4sFjrmBYWozgRwG5_GWPPAHQ7qzMAFzRks1LQe-5MZgWPJX1Q9ES0w3gchV-4FSQA0mrLi1xaIqJYbHAdiouSNHAazvV4NYTghlWNZHG8srljR-PriF6E7mwwqywkp2PzXsVj95Af7vJYCCt1OXGi1Qi4SVYvOolzL9n7Tdxw0AzsRt8EaVmyw3CqFBs21i_EbRg1hMljJv9QT8nTgrwXg/4ce/ieZUP6atQf689rw8isIxCw/h12/h001.2HZnaZMij9k7yCgZFAmFF4Lh2dcM8pwMbreKW7dwYGs)

($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) | TOP NEWS “Texas man convicted of killing protester before being pardoned now back in court, ” KVUE's Britny Eubank — “A case involving a Texas man earlier this year was back in a Travis County courtroom on Tuesday. His attorneys are fighting to throw out an indictment for a separate misdemeanor charge he's facing. Earlier this year, Gov. Greg Abbott pardoned Daniel Perry after he was convicted of murder for shooting and killing Garrett Foster during a Black Lives Matter protest in 2020. But the pardon didn't end all of his legal troubles. Perry still faces a misdemeanor charge of deadly conduct for his alleged actions on the night of the shooting leading up to Foster's death. Under Texas law, a deadly conduct charge is when a person knowingly discharges a firearm at someone or a building. If convicted of the deadly conduct charge, Perry faces up to a year in prison. However, he did spend a little more than a year in prison before he was pardoned, so the judge may take that time served into consideration. In court Tuesday, Perry's attorneys made two motions – one to quash the indictment for the misdemeanor charge and another to dismiss it. They're trying to make sure Perry is not tried for something he was already pardoned for. » This misdemeanor charge is tied to Perry's actions of driving through the crowd of protesters and shooting on July 25, 2020, not specifically Foster's death. His defense argues that because the indictment doesn't specifically name the pedestrians who were in danger in the area, the judge should scrap it. His attorneys claim they won't be able to give Perry a sufficient defense without knowing whom he's specifically charged with endangering. They also argue that without a specific identification of these pedestrians, it could open Perry up to be being charged for something he's already been tried and acquitted for. Prosecutors argue that that specific names don't matter because Perry's conduct is what put people in danger. Perry's attorneys argued back that Perry's constitutional right to be notified of these pedestrians' names so he doesn't face a double jeopardy conviction trumps whether the indictment is conduct based or not. » The judge expects to make a decision on the defense's motion to quash the indictment by the end of the week. If he decides not to quash it, there could be another hearing in this case on Jan. 9 for the motion to dismiss the indictment. In that motion to dismiss the indictment, Perry's defense is hoping to have a hearing so they can bring in David Fugitt , the lead investigator with the Austin Police Department for Perry's case, to testify. They believe his testimony will be relevant to the decision of throwing out the indictment because in his affidavit, he claimed that the District Attorney's Office tampered with him as a witness and asked him to remove exculpatory information he was planning to present to the grand jury in this case." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 12/18/2024
body:
‘Something is seriously wrong, and we’re going to try and fix it,’ APD Chief Chamberlain says in vision for future
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi folks,
I am so excited to be headed to [Turning Point's AmericaFest](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) tomorrow with our team and my two daughters! Will we see you there? Shoot us a message on social media or reply here if you'll be there, and make sure you're following along on social for live updates from Arizona!
Here's a summary of today's newsletter:
**🏔️ Here in Colorado...** Aurora Police Chief Todd Chamberlain outlined a vision for [tackling gang violence](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and crime hotspots, pledging to address these issues ethically and constitutionally. Meanwhile, Western Colorado sees a cultural clash as [socialist ideologies gain traction](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), challenging long-standing conservative values and sparking debates on housing policies. Rep.-elect Gabe Evans prepares to bring his [military experience to Congress](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), emphasizing the need for strong national security and unwavering support for Israel.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, has been indicted on murder charges as an [act of terrorism](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
**🗣️ Don't miss today's RMV original commentary...** Matt Solomon shares an inspiring story of how a small act of kindness—[collecting can tabs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—has grown into a global initiative supporting Shriners Hospitals for Children, emphasizing the power of collective generosity.
This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, December 18, 2024.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[‘Something is seriously wrong, and we’re going to try and fix it,’ APD Chief Chamberlain says in vision for future](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
It was a long night for Todd Chamberlain and the Aurora Police Department.
Oft the subject of news reports during the last year, The Edge at Lowry apartments were yet again the location of overnight police activity Tuesday, Dec. 17. This time, more than a dozen suspected Venezuelan gang members were arrested there in a home invasion. Two victims are hospitalized with non life-threatening injuries. Police are continuing to work toward identifying the suspects in custody, with their leader saying he wouldn’t sleep until they did.
“This is not an example that the city of Aurora is a gang-infested, drug-infested, crime-infested area,” said Chamberlain, Aurora’s new police chief. “There are definitely pockets that are problematic.”
He spent close to an hour Tuesday detailing the overnight stabbing, capture, taking, beating and terrorization of two persons, and the extent to which it might again be the work of Tren de Aragua gang activity, during a press conference.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

**[Schumann: Socialist seeds in conservative soil? The new battle for Western Colorado’s identity](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* In Western Colorado, socialist ideologies are gaining traction through a publication called The Revolutionist, which advocates for progressive policies and systemic change, challenging the region's conservative roots.
* The publication critiques city actions on homelessness but avoids addressing how progressive regulations, like high fees and complex approval processes, exacerbate housing shortages for families.
* The spread of socialist ideas in traditionally conservative areas raises concerns about whether free-market principles, individual liberty, and the rule of law can withstand the ideological shift.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[‘We cannot live in a world with a nuclear Iran,’ newly-elected Rep. Evans says on Middle East](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [JEWISH INSIDER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Rep.-elect Gabe Evans brings Middle East military experience to Congress, emphasizing the importance of a strong national defense and unwavering support for Israel as the U.S.’s most critical ally in the region.
* Evans advocates using every tool to prevent a nuclear Iran, maintaining Israel’s military edge, and ensuring the U.S. supports Israel’s right to self-determination without external pressures on peace agreements.
* Evans plans to prioritize public safety, border security, and addressing inflation, reflecting concerns he encountered during his campaign and legislative career in Colorado.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Grand jury indicts Luigi Mangione, 26, the suspected assassin of CEO Brian Thompson, on ‘act of terrorism’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [NATIONAL REVIEW](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, has been indicted on charges of first-degree murder in furtherance of terrorism, highlighting the calculated and intimidating nature of the crime.
* Mangione was found with a manifesto criticizing the insurance industry, a ghost gun, and fake IDs; he was arrested after a nationwide manhunt and is being held without bail.
* A poll revealed that 41% of Americans under 30 found the killing somewhat acceptable, reflecting divisive attitudes towards the act amid broader disdain for the insurance industry.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Solomon: A pop tab, a campfire and small act of kindness makes a big difference](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY MATT SOLOMON, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_Five years ago, while working with Cameron Advanced Mobility (CAM), I had an interaction around the campfire in Moab that has since garnered a global response. It highlights that in this season of giving and joy, even the most dedicated warriors embody kindness._
_CAM has been teaching tactical driving, vehicle recovery, navigation and field repairs to U.S. military, foreign military, state department groups, as well as professional off-road racing teams and civilians, for the last 20 years. This particular trip was one of CAM’s austere driver training course in Moab with members of an elite military unit just before a deployment._
_After driving the trucks all day — stopping for repairs and assisting each other in navigation or recovery when necessary — we would set up camp, build a fire, share a meal and enjoy a few beverages while telling stories and getting to know one another. This routine serves as an exercise in mental health and personal relationship building, as much as it is about teaching skills that contribute to saving the lives of our military personnel._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

**[‘Bound, pistol-whipped, beat, victimized and terrorized’: Aurora PD chief describes home invasion](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY BRIAN PORTER | LOCAL | DECEMBER 17** _More than a dozen persons were arrested overnight related to an armed home invasion in the 1200 block of Dallas St. in Aurora._ _“This is without question a gang incident,” said Police Chief Todd Chamberlain. “It could be [Tren de Aragua], it could be [a gang] other than TdA. This is gang activity and there are Venezuelans involved.”_ __
**[Boebert calls upon Senate to get her ZIP code bill to President’s desk](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN****| STATE | DECEMBER 16** _“While this may seem like a niche issue to some, it is extremely important to these cities and towns that asked for this bill to be put forward on their behalf,” Congresswoman Lauren Boebert told her colleagues on the House floor as she urged them to pass her bipartisan ZIP Code bill, H.R. 8753._
[**Rep.-elect Jeff Hurd will be a vote for energy dominance in Western Colorado**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[****](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**BY LINDY BROWNING****| STATE |****DECEMBER 16** _One of the most repeated campaign promises U.S. Rep.-elect Jeff Hurd made to the people of Colorado’s 3rd District was to pursue the Trump agenda of energy dominance._ __

**✍️ DID YOU KNOW?** Ever wondered why Americans **call a signature a "John Hancock"**? It traces back to the bold, looping cursive of the famous Founding Father, whose oversized signature on the Declaration of Independence was said to be readable without spectacles. While the declaration was approved on July 2, most historians believe it wasn’t officially signed until August 2.

clean_body: ‘Something is seriously wrong, and we’re going to try and fix it,’ APD Chief Chamberlain says in vision for future | | | | PRESENTED BY | | Hi folks, I am so excited to be headed to tomorrow with our team and my two daughters! Will we see you there? Shoot us a message on social media or reply here if you'll be there, and make sure you're following along on social for live updates from Arizona! Here's a summary of today's newsletter: 🏔️ Here in Colorado... Aurora Police Chief Todd Chamberlain outlined a vision for and crime hotspots, pledging to address these issues ethically and constitutionally. Meanwhile, Western Colorado sees a cultural clash as , challenging long-standing conservative values and sparking debates on housing policies. Rep.-elect Gabe Evans prepares to bring his , emphasizing the need for strong national security and unwavering support for Israel. 🇺🇸 On the National Stage... Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, has been indicted on murder charges as an . 🗣️ Don't miss today's RMV original commentary... Matt Solomon shares an inspiring story of how a small act of kindness——has grown into a global initiative supporting Shriners Hospitals for Children, emphasizing the power of collective generosity. This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, December 18, 2024. – Heidi Ganahl | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY BRIAN PORTER, It was a long night for Todd Chamberlain and the Aurora Police Department. Oft the subject of news reports during the last year, The Edge at Lowry apartments were yet again the location of overnight police activity Tuesday, Dec. 17. This time, more than a dozen suspected Venezuelan gang members were arrested there in a home invasion. Two victims are hospitalized with non life-threatening injuries. Police are continuing to work toward identifying the suspects in custody, with their leader saying he wouldn’t sleep until they did. “This is not an example that the city of Aurora is a gang-infested, drug-infested, crime-infested area,” said Chamberlain, Aurora’s new police chief. “There are definitely pockets that are problematic.” He spent close to an hour Tuesday detailing the overnight stabbing, capture, taking, beating and terrorization of two persons, and the extent to which it might again be the work of Tren de Aragua gang activity, during a press conference. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | In Western Colorado, socialist ideologies are gaining traction through a publication called The Revolutionist, which advocates for progressive policies and systemic change, challenging the region's conservative roots. The publication critiques city actions on homelessness but avoids addressing how progressive regulations, like high fees and complex approval processes, exacerbate housing shortages for families. The spread of socialist ideas in traditionally conservative areas raises concerns about whether free-market principles, individual liberty, and the rule of law can withstand the ideological shift. | Rep.-elect Gabe Evans brings Middle East military experience to Congress, emphasizing the importance of a strong national defense and unwavering support for Israel as the U.S.’s most critical ally in the region. Evans advocates using every tool to prevent a nuclear Iran, maintaining Israel’s military edge, and ensuring the U.S. supports Israel’s right to self-determination without external pressures on peace agreements. Evans plans to prioritize public safety, border security, and addressing inflation, reflecting concerns he encountered during his campaign and legislative career in Colorado. | Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, has been indicted on charges of first-degree murder in furtherance of terrorism, highlighting the calculated and intimidating nature of the crime. Mangione was found with a manifesto criticizing the insurance industry, a ghost gun, and fake IDs; he was arrested after a nationwide manhunt and is being held without bail. A poll revealed that 41% of Americans under 30 found the killing somewhat acceptable, reflecting divisive attitudes towards the act amid broader disdain for the insurance industry. | BY MATT SOLOMON, Five years ago, while working with Cameron Advanced Mobility (CAM), I had an interaction around the campfire in Moab that has since garnered a global response. It highlights that in this season of giving and joy, even the most dedicated warriors embody kindness. CAM has been teaching tactical driving, vehicle recovery, navigation and field repairs to U.S. military, foreign military, state department groups, as well as professional off-road racing teams and civilians, for the last 20 years. This particular trip was one of CAM’s austere driver training course in Moab with members of an elite military unit just before a deployment. After driving the trucks all day — stopping for repairs and assisting each other in navigation or recovery when necessary — we would set up camp, build a fire, share a meal and enjoy a few beverages while telling stories and getting to know one another. This routine serves as an exercise in mental health and personal relationship building, as much as it is about teaching skills that contribute to saving the lives of our military personnel. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BRIAN PORTER | LOCAL | DECEMBER 17 More than a dozen persons were arrested overnight related to an armed home invasion in the 1200 block of Dallas St. in Aurora. “This is without question a gang incident,” said Police Chief Todd Chamberlain. “It could be [Tren de Aragua], it could be [a gang] other than TdA. This is gang activity and there are Venezuelans involved.” JEN SCHUMANN| STATE | DECEMBER 16 “While this may seem like a niche issue to some, it is extremely important to these cities and towns that asked for this bill to be put forward on their behalf,” Congresswoman Lauren Boebert told her colleagues on the House floor as she urged them to pass her bipartisan ZIP Code bill, H.R. 8753. LINDY BROWNING| STATE |DECEMBER 16 One of the most repeated campaign promises U.S. Rep.-elect Jeff Hurd made to the people of Colorado’s 3rd District was to pursue the Trump agenda of energy dominance. Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat! | | ✍️ DID YOU KNOW? Ever wondered why Americans call a signature a "John Hancock"? It traces back to the bold, looping cursive of the famous Founding Father, whose oversized signature on the Declaration of Independence was said to be readable without spectacles. While the declaration was approved on July 2, most historians believe it wasn’t officially signed until August 2. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY “A dead end is just a good place to turn around.” — Naomi Judd | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. name: Wes Moore
subject: Since day one
body:
Friend,

From day one as Governor of Maryland, I set out to be the best public servant I can be. To govern with humility and grace.

If there is one thing I have learned as governor, it is that everyone has a story — including myself.
After my father passed away when I was young, I was raised by my mother and the help of the surrounding community. I learned that you are only as strong as the people that surround you.

I ended up joining the Army after earning my associate’s degree from Valley Forge Military Academy and College. It was the start of a long path toward public servitude.

In 2006, my career was jumpstarted as I became a White House fellow. I later started working in the private sector, working for a variety of charitable organizations. All of this was to give back to the communities I know well and have helped me in the past.

This all led to my decision to run for office in 2021. After volunteering for so many years and working to make a difference, I knew the time was now to step up.

After being elected in 2022, I have spent the last several years doing everything I can to govern with open ears and continue to give back to the community. I am working every day, alongside my team, to fight for a better Maryland for all of us.

It’s my mission to make this Maryland’s decade. But, this is a mission that I can’t do without the support of our grassroots community. That’s why I’m humbly asking for you to make a contribution today to support our work in making Maryland the best state it can be. Any amount will be greatly appreciated. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20241218-1?refcode=em20241218-1]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

subject: Here’s what’s on your mind:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
Your feedback is incredibly helpful as Emily gets ready to be sworn in and get to work for the people of Washington.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98906989/518207869/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.12.18_SurveyFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZWEzMzJlNC02M2JkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hYFEPKoNc2tMW37sDnQXP-S7TDpO2upZOm2K-o1b0YQ=&emci=cbcf8a23-cbbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=9ea332e4-63bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=23483442) | **Peter, if you haven’t gotten the chance yet, please complete our quick survey about what Congress should prioritize in 2025:** [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/98906990/518207870/-467321467?contactdata=EDHCzz2w0dncIFGS1syAMeQpnJ8b4Psq2hpwRJn9cYTqBDaCEPacS95gLqQnrcJ05C4ecqHY7jB7z4g0eQqFK/Q94aAGVnWkmvQKSQmDjK5A9kxOQ1ekMhi1oAxtytCSrvwy4sMpzWkBS69e9lHn/gLo+iq4JRcseQXI0dBUb8w=&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.12.18_SurveyFollowup_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZWEzMzJlNC02M2JkLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDBkM2E5ZDU4NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hYFEPKoNc2tMW37sDnQXP-S7TDpO2upZOm2K-o1b0YQ=&emci=cbcf8a23-cbbb-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=9ea332e4-63bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&ceid=23483442) We’ve already heard from many members of Team Emily:
* * *
In the House, we will need you to make sure that existing essential programs continue to be funded and to thwart Trump's egregious plans to weaponize the federal government against its own citizens.
_—Richard, Port Orchard_
* * *
Keeping the institutions of government intact; securing Social Security, Medicare, and the ACA; resisting mass deportation; protecting vulnerable communities.
_— Janine, Sequim_
* * *
Prevent the dismantling of government institutions that can respond to human needs, support scientific research (especially on global warming), and promote American leadership for peace, stability, and fairness around the world.
_— Beth, Olalla_
* * *
There will be a lot of playing 'defense' for 4 years. My spouse and I have a nice house that could quickly turn to destitution if the Affordable Care Act is repealed. We have long-term health conditions, through no fault of our own, that could make us un-insurable without the ACA. I wish you the best in defending the lives and rights of so many of my friends and neighbors.
_—Phil, Bremerton_
* * *
This is not an easy time for many people. I hope that you can prioritize women's reproductive health in particular because of the real harm that the end of Roe has caused to people in states where there are now bans.
— _Barbara, Port Townsend_
* * *
Attention to housing for all, affordable housing, mental health, reproductive rights.
_— Patricia, Tacoma_
* * *
Intense pushback on Trump's retributive agenda. As Timothy Synder reminds us in dealing with budding autocracies, 'Do not obey in advance.'
_— Peter, Shelton_
* * *

clean_body: Your feedback is incredibly helpful as Emily gets ready to be sworn in and get to work for the people of Washington. | Peter, if you haven’t gotten the chance yet, please complete our quick survey about what Congress should prioritize in 2025: We’ve already heard from many members of Team Emily: In the House, we will need you to make sure that existing essential programs continue to be funded and to thwart Trump's egregious plans to weaponize the federal government against its own citizens. —Richard, Port Orchard Keeping the institutions of government intact; securing Social Security, Medicare, and the ACA; resisting mass deportation; protecting vulnerable communities. — Janine, Sequim Prevent the dismantling of government institutions that can respond to human needs, support scientific research (especially on global warming), and promote American leadership for peace, stability, and fairness around the world. — Beth, Olalla There will be a lot of playing 'defense' for 4 years. My spouse and I have a nice house that could quickly turn to destitution if the Affordable Care Act is repealed. We have long-term health conditions, through no fault of our own, that could make us un-insurable without the ACA. I wish you the best in defending the lives and rights of so many of my friends and neighbors. —Phil, Bremerton This is not an easy time for many people. I hope that you can prioritize women's reproductive health in particular because of the real harm that the end of Roe has caused to people in states where there are now bans. — Barbara, Port Townsend Attention to housing for all, affordable housing, mental health, reproductive rights. — Patricia, Tacoma Intense pushback on Trump's retributive agenda. As Timothy Synder reminds us in dealing with budding autocracies, 'Do not obey in advance.' — Peter, Shelton Thank you for these messages — your feedback is incredibly helpful as Emily gets ready to be sworn in and get to work for the people of Washington. In service, — the whole team at Emily Randall for Congress = | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. name: Justice Democrats
subject: Trump promises mass deportations and an end to birthright citizenship.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Donald Trump isn’t even in the White House yet and he’s already pushing
his anti-immigrant agenda, including deporting U.S. citizens and
eliminating birthright citizenship.

We’re not surprised, but we are disturbed. These threats to immigrants and
their families are terrifying for many, and especially personal to Justice
Democrats’ Rep. Delia Ramirez.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241218?refcode=em20241218-36243&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36243_1203867_q4Iu1C&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C ]Tweet from Delia Ramirez: Congress has failed mixed-status families,
immigrants, and Americans for decades. I have a responsibility to inform
people and roll up my sleeves to fight for everyone in our communities.
Read the full @19thnews article

Delia is a proud daughter of immigrants, the first Latina ever elected to
Congress from Illinois, and was the only member of Congress in a
mixed-status family when her husband was undocumented.

She’s witnessed too many colleagues in the Democratic Party abandon their
values and shift to the right on issues like immigration for the sake of
scoring political points. Delia knows first-hand the impacts that
dehumanizing anti-immigrant rhetoric has on families like hers, and how
harmful these cruel policies would be to millions of people, their
communities, and our society as a whole — and she’s ready to fight back.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241218?refcode=em20241218-36243&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36243_1203867_q4Iu1C&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C ]Tweet from Delia Ramirez: Let’s be clear - Trump is posing the question
of who gets to be an American to our nation. Questioning birthright
citizenship is anti-American, and eliminating it through executive action
is unconstitutional.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241218?refcode=em20241218-36243&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36243_1203867_q4Iu1C&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C ]Help Justice Democrats support Delia and the rest of our working-class,
progressive bloc in Congress as they fight for immigrants and justice for
ALL: pitch in $5 or whatever you can to fuel our fight in
2025.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241218?refcode=em20241218-36243&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36243_1203867_q4Iu1C&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20241218-36243-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36243_1203867_q4Iu1C&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36243%2E1203867%2Eq4Iu1C

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

subject: NewsLink: Grassley rips Wray's 'failed' leadership at FBI with 11 pages of examples in blistering 'no confidence' letter
body: Background on Sen. Grassley's criticisms of FBI DIrector Wray [NEWSLINK] BREAKING NEWS... [image] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71582&L=32849&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9qdWRpY2lhbHdhdGNoYm9vay5jb20/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249bmV3c2xpbmsmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=72c92c87449bcbe2f73ea5e44e95ae366c055fbba786d3cb873e5a5accb35454] RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS IN PERIL _"When it comes to fighting for the American people’s ‘right to know,’ no one holds a candle to Tom Fitton and his team at Judicial Watch"_ - SEAN HANNITY Tom Fitton returns with an exhaustive investigation into the progressive movement’s efforts to dismantle the venerable institutions of American rights and freedoms. Order Tom Fitton's Must-Read Today! [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71582&L=32849&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9qdWRpY2lhbHdhdGNoYm9vay5jb20/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249bmV3c2xpbmsmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=72c92c87449bcbe2f73ea5e44e95ae366c055fbba786d3cb873e5a5accb35454] [600x300] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71582&L=34080&F=T&&hash=3b2feec7673e99a2e6c6e363f76d6a35bb4076f3676eb04ba2b8ab9173c5160b] Grassley rips Wray's 'failed' leadership at FBI with 11 pages of examples in blistering 'no confidence' letter [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71582&L=34080&F=T&&hash=3b2feec7673e99a2e6c6e363f76d6a35bb4076f3676eb04ba2b8ab9173c5160b] Source: Fox News [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71582&L=34080&F=T&&hash=3b2feec7673e99a2e6c6e363f76d6a35bb4076f3676eb04ba2b8ab9173c5160b] Republican Iowa Sen. Chuck Grassley doubled down that he believes Christopher Wray has failed his "fundamental duties" as FBI director in a blistering letter expressing he has "no confidence" in Wray’s continued leadership over the agency. Grassley’s lengthy letter to Wray, which spans 11 pages, detailed specific examples of Wray’s "failures" as FBI director. Order Tom Fitton's Must-Read Today! [] [600x300] [] Grassley rips Wray's 'failed' leadership at FBI with 11 pages of examples in blistering 'no confidence' letter [] Source: Fox News [] Republican Iowa Sen. Chuck Grassley doubled down that he believes Christopher Wray has failed his "fundamental duties" as FBI director in a blistering letter expressing he has "no confidence" in Wray’s continued leadership over the agency. Grassley’s lengthy letter to Wray, which spans 11 pages, detailed specific examples of Wray’s "failures" as FBI director. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] Background Notes from Judicial Watch [180x150x1] [] October 23, 2024 Judicial Watch Sues For Records on Social Media Posts Critical of Donald Trump by Top FBI Agent Investigating Trump Assassination Attempt [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch sued the Justice Department for messages among top leaders of the FBI referencing social media posts of Special Agent Jeffrey Veltri, head of the Miami Field Office, which is investigating the September 15 assassination attempt against Donald Trump. name: David Hogg
subject: Just getting started
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I’ve said this over and over because it’s super important:
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20241219-1&t=1&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)
Peter, one of the key aspects that sets Leaders We Deserve apart is that we’re playing the long game. I’ve said this over and over because it’s super important:
**Democrats need to build our bench with young progressive leaders TODAY so they can lead our country tomorrow.** A young candidate we’re electing to a state legislature right now could very well be the future President of the United States.
**[We elected six young leaders to state legislatures and Congress this year – and Peter, we’re just getting started. Pitch in $5 and help keep up the fight.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241218-1?refcode=em20241218-1540&amount=5&refcode2=1540_587540_VIAehU&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241218-1?amount=5&refcode=em20241218-1540&refcode2=1540_587540_VIAehU&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)**
In college, I studied the way conservative political movements built their power over the past 30 years, winning control of state legislatures and appointing a ton of far-right judges to advance their agenda. It didn’t happen out of nowhere – conservatives and Republicans worked tirelessly to get to where they are now.
It was a major wake-up call for me – and one of the reasons I helped start Leaders We Deserve. We have a generation of young people who are ready to step up and lead. These are the progressive leaders who will write the future of this country, who will fight for our values and create the progress that we need to see. But they need our support today to win their elections and start building those movements.
I know the results of the presidential election were extremely disappointing. Like you, I’m worried about what the future holds under a second Trump administration.
But the work we’re doing at Leaders We Deserve was always much bigger than the presidential election. I take comfort in the fact that we elected six new young people across the country – and that we’ll continue to grow that number year after year, cycle after cycle. We are building a solid foundation for a progressive future.
**[Peter, can we count on you to play the long game with us? Donate $5 now to power our work electing the next generation of progressive leaders.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20241218-1?refcode=em20241218-1540&amount=5&refcode2=1540_587540_VIAehU&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)**
Thank you!!
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20241219-1&t=2&refcode2=1540_587540_VIAehU&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=1540%2E587540%2EVIAehU)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Adam Schiff
subject: My faith
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek, with
Hanukkah beginning next week, a time to gather with friends and family, I
wanted to share a favorite throwback photo that gets circulated every time
the holiday season comes around.

Here you go. Me at 13, in all my “glory” at my Bar Mitzvah:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=dec2024_abs_em.CUeQhv&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]Adam at 13 years old before his Bar Mitzvah

I’ll admit, it’s easy to chuckle at this photo — the classic 1970s bow
tie, the braces, the yarmulke, the looks — but it is also an opportunity
to tell you a little about how I approach my faith.

Throughout my career in public service, I’ve thought a lot about the
Jewish tradition of “Tikkun Olam,” or repairing the world. And over the
last few years, there has been a lot that was broken, and a lot to fix.
It’s been my cause.

Derek, I want to
take you back to 1973. Our family moved around a lot because my father was
in the “schmata” (or clothing) business. He started as a traveling
salesman and later worked as a regional manager for a men’s slacks
company.

We moved from Massachusetts where I was born, lived a few years in
Arizona, and ended up in the Bay Area. It was there that my dad got out of
the clothing business and bought a lumber yard, where I worked during the
summers. There’s nothing like hauling sheets of plywood and roofing, and
shoveling concrete to make you want to go to graduate school.

This photo was taken when my family was a member of Temple Isaiah in
Lafayette, California, a reform synagogue we joined after moving to the
Bay Area. And it came after months of practicing for my Bar Mitzvah. I
even taped my practice sessions on our tape recorder (I have a copy of
that tape somewhere, believe it or not, voice cracking and all.)

While the Hebrew language skills I quickly acquired were just as quickly
lost, the lessons that my faith taught me endure and guide me to this day.

In fact, my favorite passage comes from Micah: What is required of us, but
to do justice, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God.

These principles have guided my work in Congress. They have led me to
pursue criminal justice reform, to make housing the homeless a key
priority, to attack discrimination, and to ensure the rule of law is
applied equally. They have also guided my path as I held accountable and
stood up to the most unscrupulous man to ever occupy the Oval Office.

And they guide me in the fight we face today, a fight I will take on from
the United States Senate: repairing and rebuilding our democracy so that
we leave it better for my children and yours.

That’s why I put everything I have into my work and for the cause of our
democracy. It’s the first thing I think about when I wake up in the
morning, and I don’t rest until I’ve done all I can for the day.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/3987?c0=dec2024_abs_em.8nS22f&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1005&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]If these priorities resonate with you, I’m asking for your help to
continue that work by chipping in $18 or another meaningful amount today.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=dec2024_abs_em.qow-zo&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1006&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/3358?c0=dec2024_abs_em.YFxOQw&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1007&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]EXPRESS DONATE $18

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=dec2024_abs_em.hc18Wf&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1008&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=dec2024_abs_em.Kw-e7E&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1009&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=dec2024_abs_em.Uyo2Ps&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1010&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=dec2024_abs_em.CUeQhv&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1011&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

Hopefully, this photo helped brighten your day and gave you a bit more
insight into why I have dedicated my life to this cause.

Thank you for reading more about me,

Adam

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=dec2024_abs_em.CUeQhv&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1012&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]Adam Schiff for Senate Banner; text Schiff to 724433

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2342?c0=dec2024_abs_em.X-rtmI&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20241218_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1013&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]DONATE MONTHY

135 E. Olive Ave., #750 Burbank CA, 91502

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=1002&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]www.adamschiff.com | [ mailto:contact@adamschiff.com ]contact@adamschiff.com | [ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=1003&akid=8223%2E5257348%2EYOU9f4 ]Privacy Policy

name: GIFFORDS
subject: Will you sign on today to show your support for protecting the ATF?
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives—more commonly known as the ATF—needs our support.
#

Friends -
The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives—more commonly known as the ATF—needs our support.
Donald Trump already has his sights set on dismantling the agency from the inside by appointing a gun lobby extremist to lead it. He’s also expected to make severe cuts to the agency’s annual budget.
The ATF plays a critical role in maintaining public safety. In particular, one of the agency’s main priorities is cracking down on illegal gun trafficking. If Donald Trump truly cares about “law and order,” one of the main tenets of his reelection campaign, he should be working to strengthen the ATF rather than weakening it.
We must work together to show that we understand the importance of the ATF and its work to keep our country safe.
[**Will you help us hold the incoming Trump administration accountable by adding your name today to show your support for protecting the ATF?**](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_atf_dec2024/?source=em241218-1-b&refcode2=9659.3935704.E4nmdr&akid=9659%2E3935704%2EE4nmdr)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_atf_dec2024/?source=em241218-1-b&refcode2=9659.3935704.E4nmdr&akid=9659%2E3935704%2EE4nmdr)**
Thank you so much for using your voice,
The team at GIFFORDS PAC

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1&akid=9659%2E3935704%2EE4nmdr) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Our healthcare system isn’t broken, it’s fixed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]

Friend,

Heartbreaking. Frustrating. Maddening. That’s the only way to describe the flood of stories I’m seeing from Americans who were denied medical insurance claims.

Healthcare is a human right.

No one should be denied the care they need, especially not in the wealthiest nation in the world.

Yet millions of Americans are uninsured, underinsured, or struggling to meet rising treatment costs while private healthcare companies rake in billions in profit every year.

We don't need to accept the high insurance company denial percentage as a matter of course. That’s why I reject their PAC and lobbying money. It’s why I’m fighting for Medicare For All.

But I need to show my colleagues that the people are with me.

Will you take action and tell Congress to guarantee healthcare for all Americans? [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/medicare-for-all-petition-2024?contactdata=ak+YDdb+LKk7DXQ+wVE/oeEgye0CqbLVlnjilnGplKvXZBYNyMq+mser/K5zlaoakWSMFhPHTzyN5VShp8W79BpzqVGI51QyREtOJ6pAwg/wGCnE28VGPDdtD33pFH0N0+LdKVNYGGXCWtCw7hqIUA==]

https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/1866873038781944073 [https://x.com/RoKhanna/status/1866873038781944073]

Nearly two-thirds of Americans [https://thehill.com/policy/healthcare/5029971-health-care-government-gallup/] support a single-payer healthcare system. The public understands that access to quality, affordable healthcare is a basic human right. Nobody should have to risk bankruptcy to get the care they need.

Sign if you agree: It’s time to listen to the American people and establish universal healthcare. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/medicare-for-all-petition-2024?contactdata=ak+YDdb+LKk7DXQ+wVE/oeEgye0CqbLVlnjilnGplKvXZBYNyMq+mser/K5zlaoakWSMFhPHTzyN5VShp8W79BpzqVGI51QyREtOJ6pAwg/wGCnE28VGPDdtD33pFH0N0+LdKVNYGGXCWtCw7hqIUA==]

Thank you for your activism and everything you do.

In solidarity,
Ro

Wait! One more thing–

Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.

If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

DONATE $3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20241218-medicare-for-all-lift&amount=3]
DONATE MORE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20241218-medicare-for-all-lift]

https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com] https://www.facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA [https://www.facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA] https://twitter.com/RoKhanna [https://twitter.com/RoKhanna] info@rokhanna.com [info@rokhanna.com]

Ro for Congress
P.O Box 3513
Santa Clara, CA 95051
United States
